title: Tests & Enemies Injuries, doubt, past trauma haunt them.
content:
Tests & Enemies Injuries, doubt, past trauma haunt them.
The Ordeal Takahashi (another player) hits rock bottom, resisting his new reality.
Resurrection They accept their pain and keep going.
Return with the Elixir Basketball isn’t just a game—it’s a way to redefine themselves.

🔥 Key Takeaways for Your Story:
• The protagonist is forced into a new world they resist.
• Their real battle isn’t external—it’s about self-acceptance.
• The journey is about redefining identity.

2.3 Activity: Story DNA Analysis

Choose three of your favorite scenes from these works and analyze them.
1. Why does this scene work?
2. What emotion does it start with? What emotion does it end with?
3. What does the protagonist learn or change?

📌 This teaches you how to craft compelling emotional moments in your own story.

Next Steps: Moving to Section 3
context: From document: The Graphic Novel Writing Guide for Derek: Inspiration, Structure… — chunk 21 (chunk 21/22)
